rubrics.3: The response draws a valid conclusion supported by the student’s data and describes two ways the student could have improved the experimental design and/or the validity of the results.
rubrics.2: The response draws a valid conclusion supported by the student’s data and describes one way the student could have improved the experimental design and/or the validity of the results. -or- The response describes two ways the student could have improved the experimental design and/or the validity of the results but fails to draw or incorrectly draws a conclusion from the student’s data.
rubrics.1: The response draws a valid conclusion supported by the student’s data but fails to describe, or incorrectly describes, how the student could have improved the experimental design and/or the validity of the results. -or- The response describes one way the student could have improved the experimental design and/or the validity of the results but fails to draw or incorrectly draws a conclusion from the student’s data.
rubrics.0: The response provides little or no correct information from the polymer investigation.

possible_conclusions: Plastic sample B has more stretchability than the other polymer plastics.
Plastic sample A has the least amount of stretchability compared to the other polymer plastics.
Not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability.
Different polymer plastics have different stretchability (and are therefore suited for different applications).
A reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors.
possible_design_improvements: Provide the before and after measurements for length (Did the samples all start out the same size?).
Make sure the samples are all of the same thickness. Variations in thickness could have caused variations in stretchability.
Perform additional trials. Some samples have similar stretchability; two trials may not be enough to be conclusive.
Indicate how many weights were added to the clamps (Was it the same number for each sample?).

question: a. Draw a conclusion based on the student’s data. b. Describe two ways the student could have improved the experimental design and/or validity of the results.
